name: Charco De La Pringue – return via the ascent of los Masegosos and Cortijos Nuevos
name_original: Charco De La Pringue – vuelta por la subida de los Masegosos y Cortijos Nuevos
region_intro: Road cycling around Sorihuela Del Guadalimar is situated within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, Spain's largest protected natural area. The region features a diverse terrain, including an orographic labyrinth of valleys, gorges, mountains, and flat lands. Cyclists encounter varied landscapes ranging from rolling olive groves and cereal fields to mountainous passes and the Guadalimar River. This area offers routes with significant elevation gains, appealing to riders seeking challenging climbs and descents. Best road cycling routes around Sorihuela Del Guadalimar The most popular road cycling route is Tranco de Beas Reservoir Loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo, a 51.8 miles (83.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive riding around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Embalse del Tranco de Beas and ascent to Mirador de Huelguecillas — circular from Hornos, a difficult 46.8 miles (75.3 km) path. This route includes an ascent to a viewpoint and passes through the historic settlement of Hornos. Local road cyclists also love the Ascent to El Yelmo — circular route around the Tranco Reservoir, a 40.3 miles (64.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 57 minutes. Road cycling around Sorihuela Del Guadalimar is defined by its location within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, its olive groves, and mountainous passes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Sorihuela Del Guadalimar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 6 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sorihuela Del Guadalimar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sorihuela Del Guadalimar?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Sorihuela Del Guadalimar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The region is known for its diverse terrain within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in this region?
A: The terrain around Sorihuela Del Guadalimar is highly varied. You'll encounter everything from rolling &lt;em&gt;olive groves&lt;/em&gt; and &lt;em&gt;cereal fields&lt;/em&gt; to challenging mountainous passes within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park. The region is characterized by an 'orographic labyrinth of valleys, calares, gorges, mountains and flat lands,' providing significant elevation gains on many routes.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in the region are challenging, there are some options suitable for beginners. The guide lists 1 easy route and 20 moderate routes. For a less strenuous experience, consider routes that stick to the flatter sections through the olive groves and along the Guadalimar River, though specific 'family-friendly' road cycling routes are less common due to the general elevation gains in the area.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by beautiful natural features. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3168204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Aceite&lt;/a&gt;, a natural pool and recreational area, or cycle around the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4508600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6044069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huelguecillas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning panoramic views of the Natural Park.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or settlements to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos&lt;/a&gt;, with its 12th-14th century castle, is a popular destination. You might also encounter the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón Árabe&lt;/a&gt; (Tower of Homage) dating back to 886 AD, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Sorihuela Del Guadalimar?
A: The road cycling routes around Sorihuela Del Guadalimar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, quiet roads, and scenic views within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several circular routes are popular in the area. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345001169/embalse-del-tranco-de-beas-and-ascent-to-mirador-de-huelguecillas-circular-from-hornos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Tranco de Beas and ascent to Mirador de Huelguecillas — circular from Hornos&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 75 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345010776/ascent-to-el-yelmo-circular-route-around-the-tranco-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Yelmo — circular route around the Tranco Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 65 km through mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sorihuela Del Guadalimar?
A: Andalusia generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be very hot, especially in the middle of the day, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are generally mild but can bring cooler temperatures to higher elevations.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads within the Natural Park. However, if you plan to venture onto specific trails or protected areas off the main roads, it's always advisable to check with the park authorities for any specific regulations or access restrictions.

Q: Where can I find amenities like cafes or accommodation in Sorihuela Del Guadalimar?
A: Sorihuela Del Guadalimar, being a village, offers basic amenities. You can find local cafes and pubs for refreshments. For accommodation, there are rural hotels and guesthouses in and around the village. Larger towns nearby may offer more extensive services, including bike shops.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its 'hard road rides' with significant elevation gains, appealing to experienced riders with very good fitness. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009289917/tranco-de-beas-reservoir-loop-from-villanueva-del-arzobispo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir Loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt;, with over 1300 meters of climbing, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345001169/embalse-del-tranco-de-beas-and-ascent-to-mirador-de-huelguecillas-circular-from-hornos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Tranco de Beas and ascent to Mirador de Huelguecillas — circular from Hornos&lt;/a&gt;, with over 1700 meters of ascent, offer substantial challenges.

Q: Can I find parking for my car when starting a road cycling route?
A: Parking is generally available in Sorihuela Del Guadalimar village. Many routes also start from nearby towns or specific points within the Natural Park where parking facilities are provided, such as near the Tranco Reservoir or other recreational areas. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.
tips: El Charco de la Pringue, is a natural pool and recreational park next to the Guadalquivir river, located in the Sierra de Segura and Cazorla. Great place to start any adventure. Free access parking.
The Tranco de Beas reservoir collects the waters of the Guadalquivir river in the Sierra de Segura. The path follows the shoreline of the reservoir, giving you spectacular views of the surroundings.
